credit: Dylan Byers first shared Meta's reversal on X, according to TechCrunch.
title: Meta removes an Instagram AI image feature after backlash over using public photos
friendlyTitle: Meta pulled an Instagram AI image feature
summary: Meta removed a Muse Image feature that let users generate images referencing public Instagram accounts by @-mentioning them. The company said the feature 'missed the mark' after criticism that people were not alerted when their public photos could be used this way.
friendlySummary: Meta removed a feature that could make AI images based on public Instagram accounts. People criticized it because users were not clearly alerted when their photos could be referenced.
whyItMatters: Consumer AI features now live inside social platforms with real identity, consent, and misuse risks. For everyday users, the lesson is to check AI privacy controls; for product teams, consent has to be designed before launch, not patched after backlash.
friendlyWhyItMatters: AI photo tools can affect real people quickly. Platforms need clear consent rules, and users should know where their privacy settings are.
